rxn_class_summary: Aminoxyl (CHAMPO)-catalyzed electrochemical 1,2-diazidation of alkenes mediated by a metastable CHAMPO-N3 charge-transfer complex as an azidyl group-transfer agent.
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes the aminoxyl radical (CHAMPO) to the oxoammonium cation (CHAMPO+), which combines with N3- to form a metastable CHAMPO-N3 charge-transfer complex; this CTC serves as the source of free N3 radical and as a radical azidyl group-transfer agent. Cathode reduces water to H2 + OH-.
electron_flow: At the anode, CHAMPO is oxidized to CHAMPO+ which associates with N3- to give a CHAMPO-N3 CTC; endergonic fragmentation releases N3• that adds to the alkene to form a β-azido C-radical (I), which then undergoes inner-sphere azidyl group transfer from a second CHAMPO-N3 to give the 1,2-diazide and regenerate CHAMPO.
rate_determining_step: Endergonic fragmentation of the CHAMPO-N3 CTC to free N3• and CHAMPO (first C-N bond formation is slower than the second).

probe_reagents.name: Vinylcyclopropane radical clock (7)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened azide 8 formed in 61% (E:Z >20:1); no direct diazidation — supports radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl-N-methallyl tosylamide (9/10)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo-trig cyclization to pyrrolidine diazide observed (11/12), consistent with radical cyclization rather than cation.

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl benzamide (13, carbocation trap)
probe_reagents.observation: Gives diazide 14 (71%) cleanly; oxazoline 15 (cation-cyclization product) not observed — excludes carbocation pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Camphor-derived alkene (16)
probe_reagents.observation: Gives only 11% diazide 17 plus 42% rearranged 1,3-azidoalcohol 18 (via norbornyl cation); shows cationic pathway operates only when carbocation is stabilized.

probe_reagents.name: 4-Methoxy-α-methylstyrene (1g)
probe_reagents.observation: Major diazide 2g (79%) plus minor azidoalcohol 20 (16%); cation capture by H2O is minor, supporting predominantly radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Pre-formed TEMPO-azidooxygenation adduct (3g/3k)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of 3 instead of alkene gave no diazide (>85% 3 recovered); rules out oxidation/mesolytic cleavage of azidooxygenation product as on-pathway.

notes: Two alternative cationic pathways were considered and disfavored: (A) direct anodic or CHAMPO+ oxidation of radical I to carbocation II followed by N3- capture, and (B) oxidation/mesolytic cleavage of the azidooxygenation byproduct 3 to II. Both are ruled out by radical clock, carbocation-trap (amide 13), and control electrolysis of 3. Cationic pathway can operate for substrates that form especially stabilized carbocations (e.g., camphor alkene 16, p-MeO styrene 1g). Catalyst design (CHAMPO vs TEMPO/ACT) suppresses β-H elimination of the oxoammonium and prolongs CTC lifetime; the dark-red color of the CTC serves as a visual UV-vis indicator of catalyst integrity.
